chunks.text: The prosecutor rose and began to lay out the detail of the case with method.
" The defendant was at the scene on the day of the crime.
This is definite from the testimony of several witness.
The defense attorney rose and argued back.
" What seems apparent is not always the truth.
We have new evidence that will prove the defendant's alibi.
On the witness stand, a young woman was trembling.
She was the person who had provided several clue to the police.
I saw the defendant run from the scene," she answered in a voice close to a whisper.
It was still too early for the judge to hand down his judgement.
The defendant was sent to jail.
But the attorney decided to appeal.
Law must be strict, but it must not lose room for mercy.
The pursuit of justice is always also a confrontation with the limits of human judgement.
